Cynara scolymus, also known as artichoke, is a perennial plant with edible flowers and buds. The leaves and flowers of Cynara scolymus have been used in traditional medicine for their health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and lowering cholesterol levels. The bioactive constituents of Cynara scolymus include cynarin, a sesquiterpene lactone responsible for its medicinal properties, as well as other flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins.
